headline: Anthropic pricing and safety disputes dominated the quarter as operational efficiency surged on momentum.
body: Operational efficiency was the standout mover, climbing to momentum 145 while AI layer and multi-tool integration weakened and tool use, MCP, inference optimization, and prompt caching all lagged. Anthropic drew sustained scrutiny: it reversed a policy that would have secretly limited Claude on frontier LLM tasks [4], faced public criticism from Redis creator Antirez [10], and users flagged the Fable model's quota cost, with one reporting 45% of a weekly quota consumed in a day [5][6]. Pricing competition intensified as OpenAI weighed cuts to retain users against Anthropic [8], while NVIDIA shipped its Nemotron 3 series pairing hybrid Mamba transformer architecture with latent mixture of experts and MTP [0].

cited_items.title: Anthropic Walks Back Policy That Could Have ‘Sabotaged’ AI Researchers Using Claude
